questions.text: Which microorganism is used to make bread rise?
questions.explanation: Yeast is a fungus that ferments sugars in dough, producing carbon dioxide gas which makes the bread rise and become fluffy.

questions.text: All microorganisms are harmful to humans.
questions.explanation: Many microorganisms are beneficial. They help in digestion, produce vitamins, make food items like yogurt and bread, and decompose waste.

questions.text: Name the process of converting sugar into alcohol using yeast.
questions.explanation: Fermentation is the metabolic process where yeast converts sugars into alcohol and carbon dioxide in the absence of oxygen.
Pathogens are microorganisms (bacteria, viruses, fungi, or protozoa) that can cause diseases in humans, animals, and plants.
